Behind triple-double from Kyle Lowry, Toronto Raptors (15-4) expanded their NBA-best record with 124-108 against Atlanta Hawks (3-15) Wednesday night.
Lowry posted points, assists, rebounds while Jonas Valanciunas added season-high points rebounds.
With Raptors maintained their one-game advantage over Milwaukee Bucks Eastern Conference.
Lowry, NBA's assist leader, also Raptors' most recent triple-double points, assists rebounds 116-112 victory over Brooklyn Nets March 2018.
32-year-old career triple-doubles with Raptors overall) while other players team history have combined
Raptors point guard left game with 4:09 remaining bloody nose nursed injury with tissues nostrils.
Pascal Siakam points) Serge Ibaka points, nine rebounds) also played pivotal roles win.
more decisive victory compared team's narrow over Orlando Magic Tuesday night.
Danny Green, made game-winning shot with seconds left against Magic, posted just points Wednesday's triumph.
This comes heels Kawhi Leonard, team's leading scorer, missing another backend back-to-back games.
Toronto games without Leonard this season Raptors head coach Nick Nurse indicated that four-time All-Star could soon play consecutive nights.
missed nine games last season quad injury which Raptors' cautious approach this season.
Thus, Raptors' four-game, four-city road trip concludes.
Toronto will enjoy Thanksgiving Thursday before returning action Friday night against slumping Washington Wizards Canada Centre.
